name: Adversarial: decision-flip sensitivity — every variable right at its threshold
rubric_notes: Every variable sits at a rubric threshold: stock_pct 0.25 (mid), cover 72h (HIGH-band boundary), delay 72h (== cover boundary, doesn't fire Rule 2 by strict >), PLATINUM at 60h (>24h so Rule 3 doesn't fire, but Rule 5 catches it as material risk at 60 < 72). Rule 5 fires partial_order. Any 1-hour perturbation in delay, cover, or PLATINUM deadline could flip the canonical action. This scenario tests decision stability: does the LLM pipeline give the same answer if the same scenario is presented twice (within stochasticity), or does the boundary-position lead to high-variance outputs? It also tests boundary-awareness: a good Arbiter should mention 'this decision is sensitive to assumptions' in the swing_condition. acceptable_actions tolerates immediate_switch (defensive switch given PLATINUM exposure on a boundary case). Confidence MEDIUM-LOW because the boundary positioning is real.
